diagrams.title: Recurrence Tree Visualization
diagrams.description: A tree diagram showing the recursive decomposition of a problem of size n into a subproblems of size n/b at each level, illustrating how the Master Theorem analyzes recursive algorithm complexity by comparing work done at each level versus total leaf work.
elements: Root node labeled 'T(n)' with problem size n
Level 1: a nodes each with problem size n/b
Level 2: a^2 nodes each with problem size n/b^2
Progressive branching until base case (n=1 or constant)
Work labels at each level: f(n), f(n/b), f(n/b^2), etc.
Number of levels: log_b(n)
Total leaves: a^(log_b(n)) = n^(log_b(a))
Color coding: different color for each level
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the recurrence structure T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n) to understand how the tree grows and where the complexity comes from

diagrams.title: Master Theorem Three Cases Comparison
diagrams.description: Three side-by-side graphs showing the relationship between n^log_b(a) (leaf work) and f(n) (root work) for each of the three Master Theorem cases, helping students understand when each case applies.
elements: Case 1 graph: f(n) grows slower than n^log_b(a) (polynomially smaller)
Case 2 graph: f(n) grows equal to n^log_b(a) (same polynomial order)
Case 3 graph: f(n) grows faster than n^log_b(a) (polynomially larger)
X-axis: n (problem size)
Y-axis: complexity T(n)
Legend showing n^log_b(a) and f(n) curves
Result labels: Θ(n^log_b(a)), Θ(n^log_b(a) log n), Θ(f(n))
Vertical dashed lines showing case boundaries
diagrams.purpose: Shows the three cases of the Master Theorem with visual comparison of the polynomial gap between leaf work and root work

diagrams.title: Master Theorem Decision Flowchart
diagrams.description: A flowchart diagram that guides users through the Master Theorem decision process, helping them determine which case applies based on comparing f(n) to n^log_b(a).
elements: Start box: 'T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n)'
Decision diamond: 'Compare n^log_b(a) and f(n)'
Branch 1: If f(n) = O(n^log_b(a-ε)) → Case 1: T(n) = Θ(n^log_b(a))
Branch 2: If f(n) = Θ(n^log_b(a)) → Case 2: T(n) = Θ(n^log_b(a) log n)
Branch 3: If f(n) = Ω(n^log_b(a+ε)) + regularity condition → Case 3: T(n) = Θ(f(n))
Annotation for Case 3 regularity: 'a·f(n/b) ≤ c·f(n) for some c<1'
Example algorithms at each case: Merge Sort (Case 2), Binary Search (Case 1), Strassen (Case 3)
diagrams.purpose: Provides a step-by-step decision guide for applying the Master Theorem to analyze recursive algorithm time complexity
